title: quercus semecarpifolia var. rufescens
intro: Quercus semecarpifolia var. rufescens is a variant of the Quercus semecarpifolia tree, commonly known as the chinquapin oak. This deciduous tree is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The bark and leaves of the Quercus semecarpifolia var. rufescens contain flavonoids and tannins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma.
